dialogue:
**Task**: Read <workspace>/ENVIRONMENT.md first вАФ it explains the test setup and how to run tests. Then read <workspace>/requirement.xml and implement the requirements. Work only in <workspace>/code/. Run tests with: docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q
**Started**: 2026-03-28 01:43:46
code_diff:
diff -ru --exclude=__pycache__ --exclude=.git --exclude=*.pyc --exclude=.dual-agent --exclude=.pytest_cache --exclude=tests src/griffe/extensions/base.py src/griffe/extensions/base.py
--- src/griffe/extensions/base.py	2026-03-28 01:22:00
+++ src/griffe/extensions/base.py	2026-03-28 01:30:06
@@ -407,7 +407,8 @@
 
     # If the import path contains a colon, we split into path and class name.
     colons = import_path.count(":")
-    if colons > 1 or (colons and ":" not in Path(import_path).drive):
+    has_drive = len(import_path) >= 2 and import_path[0].isalpha() and import_path[1] == ":"
+    if colons > 1 or (colons and not has_drive):
         import_path, extension_name = import_path.rsplit(":", 1)
     else:
         extension_name = None

requirements.location:
src/griffe/finder.py вАФ module-level (introduce new type) and functions
      `_handle_pth_file` and `_handle_editable_module`
requirements.description:
Both `_handle_pth_file` and `_handle_editable_module` currently return
      `list[Path]`. The tests call `sp.path` and `sp.always_scan_for` on every
      item in the returned list, which fails with
      `AttributeError: 'PosixPath' object has no attribute 'path'`.
      Additionally, `_handle_editable_module` raises
      `UnhandledEditableModuleError` for files whose names match the
      meson-python editable pattern (e.g. `_whatever_editable_loader.py`)
      because no corresponding pattern or handler exists.
requirements.contract:
1. Introduce a `SearchPath` named-tuple or dataclass in `finder.py` with
         at least two attributes:
           - `path: Path` вАФ the filesystem path to add to the search list.
           - `always_scan_for: str | None` вАФ if set, the finder should limit
             scanning under `path` to this package name; defaults to `None`.

      2. Change the return type of `_handle_pth_file(path: Path) ->
         list[SearchPath]`. Each path that was previously returned as a bare
         `Path` must now be wrapped in `SearchPath(path=..., always_scan_for=None)`.

      3. Change the return type of `_handle_editable_module(path: Path) ->
         list[SearchPath]`. All existing branches (editables, setuptools,
         scikit-build-core) must wrap their returned paths in `SearchPath`
         objects with `always_scan_for=None`.

      4. Add support for the meson-python editable pattern. Files whose names
         match the pattern `_<name>_editable_loader.py` (i.e. ending with
         `_editable_loader.py`) must be handled by `_handle_editable_module`
         instead of raising `UnhandledEditableModuleError`. The handler must:
           a. Parse the `install(packages, base_dir, ...)` call in the file,
              extracting the set/sequence of package names (first argument) and
              the base directory string (second argument).
           b. Compute `src_dir = Path(base_dir) / "src"`.
           c. For each package name in the set, if the directory
              `src_dir / package_name` exists on disk, return a
              `SearchPath(path=src_dir, always_scan_for=package_name)`.

      Interface summary:
        class SearchPath:
            path: Path
            always_scan_for: str | None = None

        def _handle_pth_file(path: Path) -> list[SearchPath]: ...
        def _handle_editable_module(path: Path) -> list[SearchPath]: ...

requirements.description:
The numpy docstring parser recognises standard structured sections
      (Parameters, Returns, etc.) but does not handle admonition-style sections
      such as "See Also", "Notes", "Warnings". These sections follow the same
      `Title\n------` dash-line pattern but are not in `_section_kind`, so they
      are silently absorbed into the surrounding text block instead of being
      emitted as a `DocstringSectionAdmonition`. In addition, isolated
      dash-lines that are NOT immediately preceded by a single-line title (i.e.
      `---` alone on a line) must not trigger section creation; the current
      guard `offset + 1` into `lines` can raise an `IndexError` at end-of-doc
      and also fails to distinguish a lone dash line from a true section header.
requirements.contract:
1. Extend the main parsing loop in `parse` to recognise dash-line-delimited
         sections whose title is NOT in `_section_kind`. When such a pattern is
         detected (a non-empty title line followed immediately by a dash-only
         line), the parser must:
           a. Flush any accumulated `current_section` text as a
              `DocstringSectionText` (as already done for known sections).
           b. Read the section body (all lines until the next section header or
              end of docstring) as a plain text block, stripping leading and
              trailing blank lines.
           c. Emit a `DocstringSectionAdmonition` with:
                - `title` set to the original-case title string (e.g. "See Also").
                - `kind` set to the lower-cased, space-to-hyphen-converted title
                  (e.g. "see-also", "notes", "warnings").
                - `text` set to the collected body.
              Special mappings: "warnings" вЖТ kind `"warning"`, "notes" вЖТ kind
              `"note"` (singular forms, matching MkDocs Material admonition types).

      2. A dash-line at `offset + 1` must only be treated as a section delimiter
         when `offset + 1 < len(lines)` AND the preceding line (`lines[offset]`)
         is non-empty and non-blank. Bare `---` lines with no title directly
         above them must NOT start a new section and must be appended to
         `current_section` unchanged.

      3. An admonition section with an empty body must still be emitted (with
         `text=""` / `description=""`).

      Behavioral summary:
        - "See Also\n--------\nsome text" вЖТ DocstringSectionAdmonition(title="See Also", kind="see-also", text="some text")
        - "Notes\n-----\ntext" вЖТ DocstringSectionAdmonition(title="Notes", kind="note", text="text")
        - "Warnings\n--------\ntext" вЖТ DocstringSectionAdmonition(title="Warnings", kind="warning", text="text")
        - "See Also\n--------" (empty body) вЖТ DocstringSectionAdmonition(title="See Also", kind="see-also", text="")
        - Standalone "---" line inside a text block вЖТ appended to text, no new section created.

requirements.description:
Three bugs exist in the yield-expression extraction logic:

      1. `_extract_yield` returns `repr(None)` (i.e. the string `"None"`) when
         `node.value is None` (bare `yield`). It should return the string
         `"yield"`.

      2. `_extract_yield` returns only the extracted value string when
         `node.value` is not `None` (e.g. for `yield a` it returns `"a"`).
         It should return `"yield " + extracted_value` (e.g. `"yield a"`).

      3. `ast.YieldFrom` is absent from `_node_map`, causing a `KeyError` when
         any `yield from` expression is encountered. A handler for
         `ast.YieldFrom` must be added.
requirements.contract:
1. `_extract_yield(node: ast.Yield, **kwargs) -> str`:
           - When `node.value is None`, return `"yield"`.
           - When `node.value` is not `None`, return
             `"yield " + _extract(node.value, **kwargs)`.

      2. Add `_extract_yield_from(node: ast.YieldFrom, **kwargs) -> str`:
           - Return `"yield from " + _extract(node.value, **kwargs)`.

      3. Register `ast.YieldFrom: _extract_yield_from` in `_node_map`.

      The function `get_value(node)` and `safe_get_value(node)` require no
      changes; they delegate to `_extract` which uses `_node_map`.

requirements.description:
`temporary_pypackage` accepts only an `init: bool = True` keyword
      argument that controls whether `__init__.py` is created in the leaf
      package. Tests call it with an additional `inits: bool` keyword argument,
      which raises `TypeError: temporary_pypackage() got an unexpected keyword
      argument 'inits'`. The `inits` argument is intended to control whether
      intermediate/parent namespace directories also receive `__init__.py`
      files.
requirements.contract:
Add an `inits: bool = True` keyword-only parameter to
      `temporary_pypackage`. Its semantics:
        - When `inits=True` (default), intermediate sub-directories created
          while expanding module paths receive an `__init__.py` (current
          behaviour вАФ the existing loop already calls
          `current_path.joinpath("__init__.py").touch()`).
        - When `inits=False`, intermediate sub-directories must NOT receive
          `__init__.py`, making them namespace packages.

      The existing `init` parameter continues to control whether the top-level
      leaf package directory gets `__init__.py`.

      Signature after the change:
        def temporary_pypackage(
            package: str,
            modules: Sequence[str] | Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
            *,
            init: bool = True,
            inits: bool = True,
        ) -> Iterator[TmpPackage]: ...

requirements.description:
When a `@dataclass`-labelled class has no explicitly defined `__init__`
      method (e.g. when properties force the generated `__init__` to not be
      present in the parsed members), the `parameters` property falls back to
      building a `Parameters` from `self.attributes.values()`. This collection
      includes `Attribute` members decorated with `@property` or
      `@cached_property` (which carry a `"property"` label), and those should
      not be emitted as constructor parameters.
requirements.contract:
In the dataclass fallback branch of `Class.parameters`, filter out any
      attribute whose labels include `"property"` before constructing the
      `Parameters` object.

      Expected behaviour:
        - Only attributes WITHOUT a `"property"` label are included in the
          returned `Parameters`.
        - Attributes with `"property"` in their labels (covering both
          `@property` and `@cached_property`) are excluded.

      The `try` branch (when `__init__` is found) and the empty-`Parameters()`
      fallback (non-dataclass) are unchanged.

requirements.description:
The function _load_extension currently attempts to detect whether a colon in the
      import_path string is a Windows drive-letter colon (e.g., "C:") or a class-name
      separator (e.g., "file.py:ClassName") by calling Path(import_path).drive.
      This approach is platform-dependent: on Linux, Path().drive always returns ""
      regardless of the path content, causing it to misidentify Windows-style drive
      colons as class separators when running on a POSIX host; on Windows (Python 3.12+),
      constructing Path(import_path) from a string that contains a colon in a non-drive
      position (e.g., "/path/to/file.py:ClassName") raises ValueError, preventing the
      function from executing at all.

      As a result, the two parametrize cases that use absolute POSIX paths with a
      class-name separator:
        - Path("tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest").absolute().as_posix()
      and without:
        - Path("tests/test_extensions.py").absolute().as_posix()
      either fail on Windows at the Path construction step, or may produce incorrect
      path/class splits on non-Linux platforms.
requirements.contract:
The colon-splitting logic inside _load_extension must be rewritten to use a
      platform-agnostic method for detecting Windows drive letters, without instantiating
      a Path or WindowsPath object from the unprocessed import_path string.

      Expected behavioral contract after the fix:

      1. Absolute POSIX path without class separator
         Input : "/abs/path/to/tests/test_extensions.py"
         Result: extension_name = None; the file is loaded via _load_extension_path;
                 all Extension subclasses found in the module are returned.

      2. Absolute POSIX path with class separator
         Input : "/abs/path/to/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest"
         Result: import_path = "/abs/path/to/tests/test_extensions.py",
                 extension_name = "ExtensionTest";
                 the file is loaded and the named class is instantiated.

      3. Absolute Windows path (POSIX form) without class separator
         Input : "C:/Users/user/project/tests/test_extensions.py"
         Result: extension_name = None (the single colon belongs to the drive "C:");
                 the file is loaded via _load_extension_path.

      4. Absolute Windows path (POSIX form) with class separator
         Input : "C:/Users/user/project/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest"
         Result: import_path = "C:/Users/user/project/tests/test_extensions.py",
                 extension_name = "ExtensionTest".
                 (Two colons present: one for drive, one for class separator;
                  rsplit(":", 1) correctly separates the class name from the full path.)

      The recommended detection approach: a Windows drive letter is a single ASCII
      letter immediately followed by a colon at character index 1 of the string
      (i.e., import_path[1:2] == ":"). This check works identically on all platforms
      and does not require constructing any Path object before the split.

      After the split (or decision not to split), the remaining logic вАФ checking
      os.path.exists, calling _load_extension_path, doing dynamic_import, and
      instantiating the extension class вАФ remains unchanged.
requirements.acceptance_tests:
The following test node IDs must pass after the fix:

        tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions[<absolute_path>/tests/test_extensions.py]
        tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions[<absolute_path>/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest]

      where <absolute_path> is the resolved absolute prefix produced by
      Path("tests/test_extensions.py").absolute() in the test execution environment.

      Specifically, the test cases recorded in non-passed/summary.jsonl:
        tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions[/app/codes/tests/test_extensions.py]
        tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions[/app/codes/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest]

      For each, load_extensions([extension]) must return an Extensions instance whose
      _extensions[0].__class__.__name__ equals "ExtensionTest".

      Additionally, all previously passing parametrize variants of test_loading_extensions
      must continue to pass (regression safety):
        - module dotted-path variants (with and without options dict)
        - relative file-path variants (with and without options dict)
        - relative file-path:ClassName variants (with and without options dict)
        - Extension instance and class variants
        - test_extension_events

requirements.description:
The `_load_extension` function must correctly parse absolute file paths
      (both Unix-style and Windows-style posix paths) when they are used to
      specify an extension file, including when the path is followed by a colon
      and a class name (the `filepath:ClassName` syntax).

      Currently, the colon-splitting logic in `_load_extension` must distinguish
      between:
      1. A Windows drive letter colon (e.g., `C:` at the start of a path), which
         must NOT be treated as the class-name separator.
      2. An explicit `:ClassName` suffix appended to an absolute file path, which
         IS the class-name separator.

      The failure occurs specifically for these two parametrized cases in
      `test_loading_extensions`:
      - An absolute path to a `.py` file with no class name suffix
        (e.g., `/absolute/path/tests/test_extensions.py`)
      - An absolute path to a `.py` file with a class name suffix
        (e.g., `/absolute/path/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest`)

      On Windows (using `as_posix()`), the absolute path with a class name
      contains two colons: one for the drive letter and one for the class name
      separator. The parsing logic must handle this correctly by recognising the
      drive-letter colon and splitting only at the last colon.
requirements.contract:
The function `_load_extension(extension)` must satisfy all of the
      following behavioural contracts for string `extension` values:

      (a) **Unix absolute path without class name**
          Input: `"/absolute/path/to/ext.py"`
          вАФ `extension_name` is `None`
          вАФ The file at the given path is loaded via `spec_from_file_location`
          вАФ All `Extension` subclasses found in that module are instantiated and
            returned (without options).

      (b) **Unix absolute path with class name**
          Input: `"/absolute/path/to/ext.py:SomeExtension"`
          вАФ The string is split on the last `:` into
            `import_path = "/absolute/path/to/ext.py"` and
            `extension_name = "SomeExtension"`
          вАФ The file at `import_path` is loaded
          вАФ `getattr(module, extension_name)(**options)` is returned

      (c) **Windows absolute path (posix form) without class name**
          Input: `"C:/absolute/path/to/ext.py"`
          вАФ The leading `C:` drive component must NOT be interpreted as a
            class-name separator
          вАФ `extension_name` is `None`
          вАФ The file is loaded and all `Extension` subclasses are returned

      (d) **Windows absolute path (posix form) with class name**
          Input: `"C:/absolute/path/to/ext.py:SomeExtension"`
          вАФ The string contains two colons; the split must produce
            `import_path = "C:/absolute/path/to/ext.py"` and
            `extension_name = "SomeExtension"`
          вАФ The file is loaded and `SomeExtension` is instantiated with options

      The detection of a Windows drive letter must check whether the string has
      at least 2 characters, the first character is an ASCII letter, and the
      second character is `:`.

      When `extension` is a `dict`, the same colon-parsing rules apply to the
      key of that dict after converting it to `str`.

      The loaded extension instance must be of a class whose `__name__` is
      `"ExtensionTest"` (or the specified class name) when the file contains
      that class вАФ the identity of the class object may differ from the
      in-process class because file-based loading creates a fresh module, so
      only `__class__.__name__` equality is required.
requirements.acceptance_tests:
The following test node IDs (parametrised cases of `test_loading_extensions`
      in `tests/test_extensions.py`) must pass:

      - `tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions[<absolute_path>/tests/test_extensions.py]`
        where `<absolute_path>` is the resolved absolute prefix of the project's
        `tests/` directory (i.e., `Path("tests/test_extensions.py").absolute().as_posix()`).

      - `tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions[<absolute_path>/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest]`
        where the path is `Path("tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest").absolute().as_posix()`.

      For both cases the test assertions are:
        1. `extensions._extensions[0].__class__.__name__ == "ExtensionTest"`
        2. For dict-wrapped input, `loaded.kwargs == {"option": 0}`

      All previously passing test cases in `tests/test_extensions.py` must
      continue to pass (no regression).

requirements.description:
ељУеЙНзЪДеЖТеПЈж£АжµЛйАїиЊСеЬ®е§ДзРЖеРЂжЬЙ Windows зЫШзђ¶пЉИе¶В `C:/path/file.py`пЉЙзЪДзїЭеѓєиЈѓеЊДжЧґе≠ШеЬ®ж≠ІдєЙпЉЪ
      дїЕдЊЭйЭ†"иЈѓеЊДдЄ≠жШѓеР¶еРЂжЬЙеЖТеПЈ"жЭ•еИ§жЦ≠жШѓеР¶йЬАи¶БеИЖеЙ≤з±їеРНпЉМдЉЪеѓЉиЗіе∞Ж Windows зЫШзђ¶еРОзЪД `:` иѓѓеИ§дЄЇ
      з±їеРНеИЖйЪФзђ¶пЉМињЫиАМжКК `C:/path/to/file.py` йФЩиѓѓеЬ∞жЛЖеИЖдЄЇ `import_path="C"` еТМ
      `extension_name="/path/to/file.py"`пЉМдљњеЊЧеРОзї≠ `os.path.exists` ж£АжЯ•е§±иі•пЉМ
      еєґиІ¶еПСеѓє `"C"` зЪД `dynamic_import` иАМжК•йФЩгАВ

      ж≠§е§ЦпЉМеН≥дљњеЬ® POSIX зОѓеҐГпЉИLinux/macOSпЉЙдЄЛпЉМзїЭеѓєиЈѓеЊДйЩДеК†з±їеРНпЉИе¶В
      `/absolute/path/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest`пЉЙдєЯйЬАи¶БиҐЂж≠£з°ЃиѓЖеИЂеєґеИЖеЙ≤гАВ

      ињЩдЄ§дЄ™еЬЇжЩѓж≠£еѓєеЇФдї•дЄЛдЄ§дЄ™йЭЮйАЪињЗжµЛиѓХпЉЪ
      1. зїЭеѓєиЈѓеЊДдЄНеРЂз±їеРНпЉЪ`Path("tests/test_extensions.py").absolute().as_posix()`
      2. зїЭеѓєиЈѓеЊДеРЂз±їеРНпЉЪ`Path("tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest").absolute().as_posix()`
      пЉИжµЛиѓХж≥®йЗКжШОз°ЃжМЗеЗЇпЉЪж≠§дЄ§зІНжГЕеЖµеЬ® Windows дЄКе∞§дЄЇйЗНи¶БгАВпЉЙ
requirements.contract:
`_load_extension` еЗљжХ∞еЬ®иІ£жЮР `import_path` дЄ≠зЪДеЖТеПЈжЧґпЉМењЕй°їжї°иґ≥дї•дЄЛи°МдЄЇзЇ¶еЃЪпЉЪ

      **еЖТеПЈиѓ≠дєЙеИ§жЦ≠иІДеИЩпЉИжМЙдЉШеЕИзЇІжОТеИЧпЉЙпЉЪ**

      1. ж£АжµЛиЈѓеЊДдЄ≠еЖТеПЈзЪДжАїжХ∞йЗПпЉИ`colons = import_path.count(":")`пЉЙгАВ
      2. ж£АжµЛиЈѓеЊДжШѓеР¶жРЇеЄ¶ Windows й©±еК®еЩ®е≠ЧжѓНпЉЪиЛ• `len(import_path) >= 2`пЉМдЄФ
         `import_path[0]` дЄЇ ASCII е≠ЧжѓНпЉИ`isalpha()` дЄЇзЬЯпЉЙпЉМдЄФ `import_path[1] == ":"`,
         еИЩеИ§еЃЪдЄЇеРЂзЫШзђ¶иЈѓеЊДпЉИ`has_drive = True`пЉЙгАВ
      3. еЖТеПЈеИЗеИЖеЖ≥з≠ЦпЉЪ
         - иЛ• `colons > 1`пЉИжЧ†иЃЇжШѓеР¶еРЂзЫШзђ¶пЉЙпЉМеИЩжЬАеРОдЄАдЄ™еЖТеПЈдЄЇз±їеРНеИЖйЪФзђ¶пЉМ
           дїОжЬАеРОдЄАдЄ™еЖТеПЈе§ДеИЖеЙ≤пЉИ`rsplit(":", 1)`пЉЙпЉМеЊЧеИ∞жЦЗдїґиЈѓеЊДеТМз±їеРНгАВ
           пЉИж≠§е§ДзРЖжФѓжМБ `C:/path/file.py:ClassName` ињЩзІН Windows иЈѓеЊДйЩДеК†з±їеРНзЪДжГЕеЖµгАВпЉЙ
         - иЛ• `colons == 1` дЄФ `has_drive == False`пЉМеИЩиѓ•еЖТеПЈдЄЇз±їеРНеИЖйЪФзђ¶пЉИPOSIX иЈѓеЊДйЩДеК†з±їеРНпЉЙпЉМ
           дїОиѓ•еЖТеПЈе§ДеИЖеЙ≤гАВ
         - иЛ• `colons == 1` дЄФ `has_drive == True`пЉМеИЩиѓ•еФѓдЄАеЖТеПЈдЄЇй©±еК®еЩ®зЫШзђ¶пЉМдЄНиІ¶еПСеИЖеЙ≤пЉМ
           `extension_name = None`гАВ
         - иЛ• `colons == 0`пЉМеИЩдЄНиІ¶еПСеИЖеЙ≤пЉМ`extension_name = None`гАВ

      **жЦЗдїґиЈѓеЊДеК†иљљиІДеИЩпЉИдЄОеЖТеПЈе§ДзРЖзЫЄзїУеРИпЉЙпЉЪ**
      - ж≠£з°ЃиІ£жЮРеЗЇ `import_path` еРОпЉМиЛ• `os.path.exists(import_path)` дЄЇ `True`пЉМ
        еИЩйАЪињЗ `spec_from_file_location` дїОжЦЗдїґеК†иљљж®°еЭЧпЉМиАМйЭЮйАЪињЗ `dynamic_import`гАВ
      - иЛ• `extension_name` йЭЮз©ЇпЉМеИЩдїОеЈ≤еК†иљљж®°еЭЧдЄ≠йАЪињЗ `getattr(module, extension_name)` иОЈеПЦз±їпЉМ
        дљњзФ® `options` еЃЮдЊЛеМЦеРОињФеЫЮгАВ
      - иЛ• `extension_name` дЄЇ `None`пЉМеИЩйБНеОЖж®°еЭЧдЄ≠жЙАжЬЙеѓєи±°пЉМжЙЊеЗЇжЙАжЬЙжї°иґ≥
        `isclass(obj) and issubclass(obj, ext_classes) and obj not in ext_classes` зЪДз±їпЉМ
        йАРдЄАдљњзФ® `options` еЃЮдЊЛеМЦеРОдї•еИЧи°®ељҐеЉПињФеЫЮгАВ

      **еЗљжХ∞з≠ЊеРНдЄНеПШпЉЪ**
      ```python
      def _load_extension(
          extension: str | dict[str, Any] | ExtensionType | type[ExtensionType],
      ) -> ExtensionType | list[ExtensionType]:
      ```

      **еЕ≥йФЃиЊєзХМжЭ°дїґпЉЪ**
      - POSIX зїЭеѓєиЈѓеЊДпЉИе¶В `/app/codes/tests/test_extensions.py`пЉЙпЉЪ`colons=0`пЉМ
        `extension_name=None`пЉМжЦЗдїґе≠ШеЬ®жЧґдїОжЦЗдїґеК†иљљпЉМеПСзО∞жЙАжЬЙжЙ©е±Хз±їгАВ
      - POSIX зїЭеѓєиЈѓеЊДйЩДеК†з±їеРНпЉИе¶В `/app/codes/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest`пЉЙпЉЪ
        `colons=1`пЉМ`has_drive=False`пЉМдїОеЖТеПЈе§ДеИЖеЙ≤пЉМ`import_path` дЄЇжЦЗдїґиЈѓеЊДпЉМ
        `extension_name` дЄЇ `"ExtensionTest"`гАВ
      - Windows зїЭеѓєиЈѓеЊДжЧ†з±їеРНпЉИе¶В `C:/path/tests/test_extensions.py`пЉЙпЉЪ`colons=1`пЉМ
        `has_drive=True`пЉМдЄНеИЖеЙ≤пЉМ`extension_name=None`пЉМжЦЗдїґе≠ШеЬ®жЧґдїОжЦЗдїґеК†иљљгАВ
      - Windows зїЭеѓєиЈѓеЊДйЩДеК†з±їеРНпЉИе¶В `C:/path/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest`пЉЙпЉЪ
        `colons=2`пЉМдїОжЬАеРОдЄАдЄ™еЖТеПЈе§ДеИЖеЙ≤пЉМж≠£з°ЃеЊЧеИ∞жЦЗдїґиЈѓеЊДеТМ `"ExtensionTest"`гАВ
requirements.acceptance_tests:
дї•дЄЛйЭЮйАЪињЗжµЛиѓХзФ®дЊЛеЬ®дњЃжФєеРОењЕй°їйАЪињЗпЉЪ

      1. `tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions[/app/codes/tests/test_extensions.py]`
         вАФ еѓєеЇФеПВжХ∞ `Path("tests/test_extensions.py").absolute().as_posix()`пЉИзїЭеѓєиЈѓеЊДпЉМжЧ†з±їеРНпЉЙ
         вАФ жЦ≠и®АпЉЪ`extensions._extensions[0].__class__.__name__ == "ExtensionTest"`

      2. `tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions[/app/codes/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest]`
         вАФ еѓєеЇФеПВжХ∞ `Path("tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest").absolute().as_posix()`пЉИзїЭеѓєиЈѓеЊДпЉМеРЂз±їеРНпЉЙ
         вАФ жЦ≠и®АпЉЪ`extensions._extensions[0].__class__.__name__ == "ExtensionTest"`

      еРМжЧґпЉМдї•дЄЛзО∞жЬЙзФ®дЊЛдЄНеЊЧеЗЇзО∞еЫЮељТе§±иі•пЉЪ
      - `tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions[tests.test_extensions]`
      - `tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions[tests/test_extensions.py]`
      - `tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions[tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest]`
      - `tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions[extension0]`пЉИExtensionTest еЃЮдЊЛпЉЙ
      - `tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions[extension1]`пЉИExtensionTest з±їпЉЙ

requirements.description:
The two non-passed test cases exercise `load_extensions` with absolute file paths
      generated by:
        (a) Path("tests/test_extensions.py").absolute().as_posix()
            вЖТ e.g. "/app/codes/tests/test_extensions.py"  (no class name suffix)
        (b) Path("tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest").absolute().as_posix()
            вЖТ e.g. "/app/codes/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest"  (class name suffix)

      The `_load_extension` function must handle the `:ClassName` suffix while
      correctly treating a Windows drive-letter colon (e.g. `C:`) as part of the
      path, not as a class-name separator.  Without this distinction, a Windows
      path such as `C:/path/file.py` would be incorrectly split and the resulting
      path `C` would not exist on the filesystem, causing an `ExtensionNotLoadedError`.

      The logic must cover four input shapes:
        - POSIX absolute path, no class name:  `/abs/path/file.py`
        - POSIX absolute path + class name:    `/abs/path/file.py:ClassName`
        - Windows absolute path, no class name: `C:/abs/path/file.py`
        - Windows absolute path + class name:  `C:/abs/path/file.py:ClassName`
requirements.contract:
Within `_load_extension`, after resolving `import_path` (a plain string or the
      key from a dict specification) and before the `os.path.exists` check, the
      following colon-detection contract must hold:

      1. Compute `colons = import_path.count(":")`.

      2. Compute `has_drive` as True if and only if:
           len(import_path) >= 2
           AND import_path[0] is an ASCII letter (isalpha())
           AND import_path[1] == ":"
         This identifies a Windows drive-letter prefix such as "C:".

      3. Determine whether to split off a class name:
           Split condition: (colons > 1) OR (colons == 1 AND NOT has_drive)
           - `colons > 1`: handles Windows path WITH class suffix (2 colons total).
             Use rsplit(":", 1) so only the last colon is used as separator.
           - `colons == 1 AND NOT has_drive`: handles POSIX absolute path WITH class
             suffix (1 colon that is not a drive letter).
           If the split condition is False (0 colons, or 1 colon that is a drive
           letter), set `extension_name = None` and keep `import_path` unchanged.

      4. After the split, if `os.path.exists(import_path)` is True:
           - Call `_load_extension_path(import_path)` to load the module.
           - If `extension_name` is not None:
               Return `getattr(module, extension_name)(**options)`.
               Raise `ExtensionNotLoadedError` if the attribute does not exist.
           - If `extension_name` is None:
               Collect all classes in `vars(module)` that are subclasses of
               `(VisitorExtension, InspectorExtension, Extension)` but are NOT
               identical to any of those three base classes.
               Return a list of instances, each constructed with `**options`.

      5. `_load_extension_path(path: str) -> ModuleType` must accept and correctly
         process an absolute path string (not only relative paths).
requirements.acceptance_tests:
The following two parametrised cases of
      `tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions` must pass.  The exact
      pytest node IDs are environment-dependent (the absolute path prefix equals
      `Path("tests/test_extensions.py").absolute().parent`), but in the CI target
      environment they are:

        tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions[/app/codes/tests/test_extensions.py]
        tests/test_extensions.py::test_loading_extensions[/app/codes/tests/test_extensions.py:ExtensionTest]

      For each, the assertion that must succeed is:
        extensions = load_extensions([extension])
        loaded = extensions._extensions[0]
        assert loaded.__class__.__name__ == "ExtensionTest"

      All other parametrised cases of `test_loading_extensions` (relative path,
      dotted module name, dict form, instance, class) and `test_extension_events`
      must continue to pass without regression.
